title: Masala Omelette

overview.description: A fluffy Indian omelette packed with onion, green chilli, coriander and warming spices, cooked until lightly golden.

method: Beat the eggs until well combined.
Stir in the onion, chilli, coriander, cumin seeds, turmeric, chilli powder if using, salt and black pepper.
Heat the ghee in a frying pan over a medium heat.
Pour in half the egg mixture.
Cook gently until the underside is golden and the top is almost set.
Fold the omelette in half and cook for another 30–60 seconds.

learning.did-you-know: Masala omelettes are a favourite at roadside tea stalls across India, where they're often served with buttered toast and a cup of chai.
learning.what-you-will-learn: How fresh herbs, spices and aromatics can completely transform a simple omelette.

notes.why-its-in-the-chapman-kitchen: A quick, inexpensive breakfast using ingredients that are almost always available in the Chapman Kitchen.
chapman-tips: Dice the onion very finely so it softens as the eggs cook.
Beat the eggs thoroughly for a lighter omelette.
Cook over a medium heat to avoid overcooking the eggs.
Ghee adds a wonderful flavour, but neutral oil also works well.
If you've got leftover cooked potato, crumble a small handful into the eggs before cooking. It's a common variation that makes the omelette even more filling without needing another recipe.
